Motion To Reopen And To Compel Discovery
Off Calendar Aug-17-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Wm J. Hapiuk of Herrler Ehrman on behalf of Defendant/moving party Ernst & Young. and Joel F. Donahoe, Esquire, on behalf of Plaintiff.

TENTATIVE RULING IS ADOPTED BY THE COURT - RESPOND WITHIN 20 DAYS.
Defendant Ernest & Young's Motion To Reopen And To Compel Discovery is granted in part and denied in part. The motion is granted as it seeks to propound further written discovery related to the response to Interrogatory No. 4 and updates to previous responses and document production. This portion of the motion is granted due to the length of time that has elapsed between the previous trial date and the present trial date. The motion is denied as it seeks to take a second deposition of Plaintiff and Irving Alameda. The court does not find good cause for a second deposition of either witness.
COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT (612)
